label: Request Data from User
description: Pause the workflow and ask the user to fill a typed form, then resume with their answers. Unlike human_approval (which only yields approve/reject), human_input collects actual values mid-run — a missing parameter, a decision the model can't make, or a credential to bind. Downstream nodes read the answers via {{steps.<id>.output.values.<fieldName>}}.

settings.name: fields
settings.label: Form fields
settings.description: Array of field descriptors the user must fill. Each: { name, label, type (string|number|enum|secret|boolean|file|date|json), required (bool), options? (string[] for enum), default?, placeholder?, validation? (regex string or {min,max}) }. secret-typed fields write to the workflow secret vault and never appear in execution data.

settings.name: title
settings.label: Form title
settings.placeholder: We need a bit more information to continue
settings.description: Heading shown above the form. Templating is supported (e.g. reference an upstream value).

settings.name: description
settings.label: Form description
settings.placeholder: Please provide the following so the flow can proceed.
settings.description: Explanatory text shown under the title. Templating is supported.

settings.name: timeout
settings.label: Timeout (seconds)
settings.description: How long to wait for the user to submit before applying timeoutAction (default 24h).

settings.name: timeoutAction
settings.label: Timeout action
settings.description: What to do when the timeout elapses without a submission. 'fail' aborts the run (fail-closed); 'use_default' resumes using each field's configured default (only valid if every required field has a default).

settings.name: assignTo
settings.label: Assign to
settings.description: Array of user ids or group names allowed to answer. Defaults to the user who started the run.

settings.name: channel
settings.label: Channel
settings.description: Where to surface the form. 'chat' renders it inline in the conversation; 'in_app' on the flows canvas; email/slack send a link.

ai.shortDescription: Pause and ask the user for typed input mid-run, then resume.
ai.whenToUse: When a node needs a value that cannot be pre-collected at author time — a per-run decision (which framework? which schema?), a parameter derived from earlier nodes, or a credential the tenant must bind. Insert this so the flow asks the user exactly what's missing instead of guessing or failing.
ai.examplePrompt: before generating the SQL, ask the user which schema to query
ai.promptHints: Read answers via {{steps.<id>.output.values.<fieldName>}}. Use required:true with no default to force an answer. Prefer this over inventing a default for a required field.

outputAssertions.name: non_empty_fields
outputAssertions.errorMessage: human_input node has no fields[] — the user would see an empty form. Configure at least one field.
